title: Kinematic Flow and Differential Equations for Cosmological Correlators
speaker: Austin Joyce

proof_stages.label: Cosmological correlators as kinematic wavefunction coefficients
proof_stages.summary: The talk frames cosmological observables as functions of momentum polygons and then refines them to wavefunctional coefficient kernels carrying the same correlation data.

proof_stages.label: Power-law FRW φ³ toy model and Feynman rules
proof_stages.summary: A conformally coupled scalar field with φ³ interaction in a power-law cosmological metric supplies the explicit perturbative setting and the time-integral Feynman rules for wavefunction coefficients.

proof_stages.label: Flat-space seed and nonoverlapping-circles construction
proof_stages.summary: Specializing to ε=-1 produces rational flat-space wavefunctions computable by summing legal circlings of graphs, interpretable as canonical functions of positive geometries and extendable to sums over triangulations.

proof_stages.label: Kinematic singularities, regular folded limits, and factorization
proof_stages.summary: The flat seed has poles when total or subgraph energy vanishes, with residues giving scattering amplitudes or lower wavefunctions, while real folded configurations must remain regular and later serve as boundary conditions.

proof_stages.label: Cosmological deformation as Euler-type integrals and ε-form systems
proof_stages.summary: Fourier transforming the time-dependent coupling writes the FRW wavefunction as a twisted Euler integral over shifted flat-space wavefunctions, so hyperplane-arrangement integration-by-parts yields finite master-integral bases and first-order differential equations.

proof_stages.label: Marked-graph kinematic flow for the first-order differential
proof_stages.summary: The large differential systems are compressed by encoding letters and basis functions as marked-graph circlings and by a growth/flow rule whose tree directly reads off each differential.

proof_stages.label: Nilpotency, locality, and unexpectedly low-order equations
proof_stages.summary: The graphical differential is consistent because kinematic letters obey locality-driven three-term identities and rigid sign assignments, and the physical wavefunctions satisfy lower-order equations inherited from spacetime wave equations.

proof_stages.label: Combinatorial reformulation, open directions, and deformations
proof_stages.summary: The talk closes by asking for an intrinsic kinematic/combinatorial derivation, a classification of solution functions, extensions to summed triangulations and massive/deformed models, and methods for loop-momentum integrations.
